data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture. Let me identify all the legacy patterns and modernize this project.

Legacy patterns found:
1. **Old-style `<Project>` element** with `ToolsVersion` and XML namespace — SDK-style uses `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">`
2. **Explicit `<Import>` of `Microsoft.Common.props` and `Microsoft.CSharp.targets`** — SDK-style handles this automatically
3. **Boilerplate `<PropertyGroup>`** with `Configuration`, `Platform`, `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects`, `Deterministic` — all defaults in SDK-style


4. **Separate Debug/Release `<PropertyGroup>` blocks** — SDK-style provides sensible defaults for these
5. **Explicit `<Reference>` items for System assemblies** — SDK-style includes these implicitly for .NET Framework
6. **Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items** — SDK-style uses glob patterns by default (all `.cs` files are included)
7. **`Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs`** — SDK-style auto-generates assembly attributes; metadata can be specified as MSBuild properties
8. **`TargetFrameworkVersion` v4.7.2** — SDK-style uses `<TargetFramework>` instead

, with the TFM `net472` (or we could suggest upgrading to .NET 6/8)

Now I'm creating a minimal SDK-style project file that consolidates all this metadata into a single `<PropertyGroup>` with just the essential properties like target framework, namespace, assembly name, and version information.

Actually, I realize I can trim this down even further since the assembly name and root namespace will default to the project filename `LegacyApp`, and `OutputType` defaults to `Library` anyway. So I should remove those redundant properties and then clean up the project by deleting the `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` file, removing all the explicit `<Compile>` item declarations since SDK-style automatically globs all `.cs` files, and stripping out the rest of the boilerplate. Let me start making those changes.

</Project>

</Project>

data.content:
Here's a summary of every legacy pattern found and how it was resolved:

| Legacy Pattern | Resolution |
|---|---|
| `ToolsVersion="15.0"` + XML namespace | Replaced with `Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"` |
| Explicit `<Import>` of `Microsoft.Common.props` / `Microsoft.CSharp.targets` | Implicit in SDK-style |
| `Configuration`, `Platform`, `ProjectGuid`, `FileAlignment`, `Deterministic` | SDK defaults — removed |
| `OutputType Library` | Default for SDK class libraries — removed |
| `RootNamespace` / `AssemblyName` matching project filename | Inferred from `.csproj` name — removed |
| `AutoGenerateBindingRedirects` | SDK default for .NET Framework — removed |
| Debug/Release `PropertyGroup` blocks (optimize, output path, debug type, constants, warning level) | All SDK defaults — removed |
| Explicit `<Reference>` for System assemblies | Implicit for `net472` — removed |
| Explicit `<Compile Include="...">` items | SDK globs all `*.cs` automatically — removed |
| `Properties/AssemblyInfo.cs` | Deleted; metadata moved to MSBuild properties (`Description`, `Company`, `Copyright`, `Version`) which auto-generate assembly attributes |
| `TargetFrameworkVersion v4.7.2` | Replaced with TFM `net472` |

**Result:** 47 lines → 11 lines. The two `.cs` source files are unchanged.
